codeReview.summary: The committed JsonRepository CRUD implementation is largely complete, but the remove_* methods do not satisfy the feature contract because they leave related records and indexes untouched. Tests in this feature also miss these regressions and include one invalid cross-area scenario.

issues.description: remove_character only removes the Character itself. The feature contract for json-repo-crud requires remove operations to keep CacheManager indexes consistent in real time (VAL-IDX-005/006 coverage called out in the handoff), but removing a character that has facts/deductions/rejections leaves those records behind and leaves stale triples in fact_index/pending_index/rejection_index until the later cascade feature. That means the repository state after a CRUD remove is internally inconsistent for this feature's own expectedBehavior.

remove_location has the same correctness gap as remove_character: it deletes the Location but leaves any referencing Fact/Deduction/Rejection records and dedup indexes untouched. This violates the repository's stated cache-coherence invariant for mutations and can incorrectly block future deductions with stale triples after a location delete.

remove_time_slot removes the TimeSlot entry but does not clean referencing facts/deductions/rejections or dedup indexes. Even before explicit cascade-delete support was added later, this breaks the feature's own index-consistency expectations because get_time_slot_by_id() returns None while stale triples involving the deleted time slot still remain in cache-backed dedup sets.

The CRUD test coverage for remove_character/remove_location/remove_time_slot only asserts the entity list shrinks. It never verifies that related repository indexes and dependent records remain coherent after removal, which is why the stale-reference bug above shipped undetected.

test_save_reload_preserves_all_data adds a fact using ts.id + '_dummy', which is not a valid existing time-slot ID. The test still passes because add_fact performs no referential validation, so this scenario does not actually verify a valid persistence round-trip and weakens the claimed cross-area coverage.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The refactor-worker skill says to compare expected behavior to architecture invariants and verify cache invalidation after representative mutations, but this feature still shipped remove_* methods that left stale related records/indexes. The skill may need a stronger explicit checkpoint to inspect removal-side cache coherence, not just add/update flows.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SKILL.md Step 5 asks workers to review 'Missing cache invalidation after mutations'; commit d252752 remove_character/remove_location/remove_time_slot only invalidate the removed entity, while tests/test_json_repository.py lacks assertions for dependent-record/index cleanup after removals.

sharedStateObservations.observation: This mission's architecture doc already states the invariant that repository mutations must maintain cache coherence and mentions cascade delete as a key improvement, but the feature handoff claims VAL-IDX coverage without noting that remove_* semantics remained inconsistent until the follow-up cascade feature. A short note in mission guidance clarifying that pre-cascade remove operations still need coherent cache state would likely prevent this misread.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md invariant #2 says every mutation must avoid stale indexes, while commit d252752 remove_* methods leave referenced fact/deduction/rejection data untouched and tests do not check for stale dedup entries after removals.

summary: Review failed. JsonRepository CRUD was implemented, but in commit d252752 the remove_character, remove_location, and remove_time_slot methods left stale dependent records and dedup indexes, so the repository could enter an internally inconsistent state after removals. The accompanying tests missed this by only checking entity deletion and by including one invalid persistence scenario.
